metadata.description: Helper skills for the claurke system. claurke-onboarding walks new users through fresh install. claurke-ops surfaces operational knowledge after install (troubleshooting, layering model, decision log, etc.) and ships session-close, the universal session-close protocol for any claurke project without a dedicated close skill; session-close-wherehouse, its dev variant that adds Linear/git close gates; linear-ops, standalone Linear board management for JAD-tracked dev work; plus spec-shard-with-contracts, which shards a locked plan into resumable spec docs carrying explicit inter-shard contracts. Install onboarding first; install ops once you have the system running.

plugins.name: claurke-onboarding
plugins.description: Walks new users through installing the claurke system. Use after running the install.sh one-liner from a terminal. The skill interviews you with multiple-choice questions for voice preferences and personal preferences, creates your private overlay repo, populates voice-profile.md and personal-preferences.md from your answers, and surfaces the final manual Cowork settings steps. Triggered by phrases like 'install claurke for me', 'set up claurke', 'onboard me to claurke'.

plugins.name: claurke-ops
plugins.description: Surfaces operational knowledge about the claurke system once it is installed - layering model, troubleshooting, update workflows, recovery scenarios, decision log, implementation gotchas for kit maintainers. Triggered automatically when users ask operational questions about their claurke setup. Also ships session-close, the universal session-close protocol (AskUserQuestion close interview, STATUS.md update + prune to STATUS-archive.md, three-category memory writes, gotcha candidates for approval, conditional PRIMER.md refresh, copy-paste next-session kickoff prompt) for any claurke project without a dedicated close skill; session-close-wherehouse, the dev variant that delegates that universal layer and adds Linear board reconcile + git loose-ends gates; linear-ops, standalone Linear board management (JAD ticket conventions, state reconcile, board hygiene, map shipped PRs to Done with receipts) triggerable any time; spec-shard-with-contracts, which decomposes a locked master plan into resumable spec docs each carrying an explicit contract (assumes-done / guarantees-for-next / validation) for long-running, multi-session builds; and new-project, the interview-driven scaffolder for new claurke projects and nested sub-workspaces (runs scripts/new-project.sh headlessly in Claude Code, scaffolds via file tools in Cowork).
